title: Clock, Delta Time, Fixed Steps, Interpolation, and Stalls · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers clock, delta time, fixed steps, interpolation, and stalls into a changed bounded deterministic time-step policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded deterministic time-step policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.
Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.
Accumulate real time into bounded fixed simulation updates and preserve remainder for interpolation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Clock Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 2263 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Clock Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for call clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Clock Contract, keep Clock Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Fixed Step · changed case · Lab Fixed Step
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 5068 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Time Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-time-evidence-15qql2" that uses case 3339 to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Time Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Time Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Time Evidence, keep Time Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-time-evidence-15qql2
# Competency: asteroids-time-evidence.
# Case 3339: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_time_evidence_15qql2(frame_seconds: float = 0.016, max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1, fixed_step: float = 1 / 120, accumulator: float = 0.0, max_updates: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_15qql2 = "15qql2-2lqq51-3339"
    assert evidence_variant_15qql2
    evidence_axes_15qql2 = (
        "player15qql2",
        "seed3339",
        "input2lqq51",
        "frame15qql23339",
        "state333915qql2",
        "playtest2lqq513339",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_15qql2)) == 6
    if frame_seconds < 0.0 or fixed_step <= 0.0:
        return False, frame_seconds, "time-domain"
    clamped = min(frame_seconds, max_frame_seconds)
    updates = int((accumulator + clamped) / fixed_step)
    if updates > max_updates:
        return False, updates, "spiral-guard"
    if clamped > max_frame_seconds:
        return False, clamped, "clamp-failure"
    assert 0 <= updates <= max_updates
    return True, updates, "bounded-time-step"
